lede: A look at November 6 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Suleiman the Magnificent and Ana Ivanovic, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Syrian civil war
events.desc: Syrian civil war: The Syrian Democratic Forces launch an offensive to capture the ISIL-held city of Raqqa.

events.title: Tammy Baldwin
events.desc: Tammy Baldwin becomes the first openly gay politician to be elected to the United States Senate.

events.title: 1988 Lancang–Gengma earthquakes
events.desc: Lancang–Gengma earthquakes: At least 730 are killed after two powerful earthquakes rock the China–Myanmar border in Yunnan Province.

events.title: Kelly Barnes Dam
events.desc: The Kelly Barnes Dam, located above Toccoa Falls College near Toccoa, Georgia, fails, killing 39.

events.title: Nguyễn Ngọc Thơ
events.desc: Nguyễn Ngọc Thơ is appointed to head the South Vietnamese government by General Dương Văn Minh's junta, five days after the latter deposed and assassinated President Ngô Đình Diệm.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The 1st Ukrainian Front liberates Kyiv from German occupation.

events.title: Spanish Civil War
events.desc: Spanish Civil War: The republican government flees from Madrid to Valencia, leading to the formation of the Madrid Defense Council in its stead.

events.title: William McKinley
events.desc: President William McKinley is re-elected, along with his vice-presidential running mate, Governor Theodore Roosevelt of New York.

events.title: Abraham Lincoln
events.desc: Abraham Lincoln is elected the 16th president of the United States with only 40% of the popular vote, defeating John C. Breckinridge, John Bell, and Stephen A. Douglas in a four-way race.

events.title: Walls of Constantinople
events.desc: A powerful earthquake destroys large portions of the Walls of Constantinople, including 57 towers.

births.name: Charles II of Spain
births.line: last Habsburg ruler of the Spanish Empire

births.name: Mike Nichols
births.line: German-born American actor, director, producer, and screenwriter

deaths.text: James Watson, American molecular biologist, geneticist, and zoologist
Antoni Martí, Andorran politician, former Prime Minister of Andorra

observances.text: Gustavus Adolphus Day (in Sweden, Finland and Estonia)
